headline: MoE-certified — C1+C2 on real weights across all 26 MoE layers; ADR-007 partial L1
headline_note: Per ADR-007 the bare word 'certified' is forbidden for MoE. C1 (per-expert) and C2 (router top-k set equality) are PASS on the REAL DeepSeek-V2-Lite weights across ALL 26 MoE layers x 64 routed experts = 1664 experts, vs a float64 reference computed one expert at a time (dense-first layer 0 skipped). C3 (MLA attention) is satisfied at the MECHANISM level (DeepSeek MLA cert tiny 9.999e-6 + MLA-0 V2-Lite-like full forward 9.072e-5), the same standard Qwen-MoE used for its C3. This is the L1 work, with the C3-mechanism caveat; it is NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED, and L2 (C4 fold) / L3 (C5 active-path) remain pending.

C1_per_expert.scope: ALL 26 MoE layers (layers 1-26; dense layer 0 skipped) x 64 routed experts = 1664 experts (exhaustive)
C1_per_expert.weights: real trained DeepSeek-V2-Lite weights (bf16; 3 MoE layers span two shards)
C1_per_expert.oracle: NumPy/torch float64, one expert at a time, one MoE layer at a time (no full-model F64)
C1_per_expert.metric: max_abs_diff(Atenia expert forward, F64 reference) per expert
C1_per_expert.gate: max_abs_diff < 0.5 for EVERY expert (ADR-004 bar, unchanged)
C1_per_expert.result: all 1664 experts < 0.5 (global worst ~1.9e-6, ~2.6e5x inside the gate)
C1_per_expert.shared_expert_note: the 2 always-on ungated shared experts are part of the MoE block convention (C3/mechanism); C1 here covers the 64 routed experts per layer.

C2_router.hard_gate: top-6 expert SET EQUALITY vs the F64 reference, every MoE layer
C2_router.routing_margin_note: gap between the 6th and 7th router logit per layer. norm_topk_prob=false (no renorm) does not affect the top-k SET (softmax monotonic); the tightest layer (23, margin 0.0120) still matched exactly.
C2_router.result: top-6 set identical on all 26 MoE layers

C3_attention.evidence: DeepSeek MLA attention cert (layer-0, 9.999e-06 vs HF, MOE-FULL-12) + MLA-0 V2-Lite-like full forward (9.072e-05 vs HF, YaRN + dense-first)
C3_attention.caveat: certifies the MLA attention + YaRN MECHANISM (tiny fixtures), NOT the real-weight per-layer attention re-certified. Same standard as Qwen-MoE C3. Recorded, not over-claimed.

C4_assembly_topology.evidence: deepseek_scale topology cert 7.806e-03 (16-routed/top-6/2-shared/MLA, MOE-FULL-15; f32-vs-f64, argmax exact)
C4_assembly_topology.note: folding C4 -> L2 is the next milestone; the scale fixture is random-weight topology, not the real trained weights.

C5_active_path.blocker: real-weight full forward (MLA + YaRN + dense-first + MoE) vs F64 one-layer-at-a-time, not yet run (MLA-1 C5 / a later phase).

pending_levels.blocker: fold C4 (deepseek_scale topology, available) with this C1+C2+C3.
C5 active-path full-forward on the real weights.
global model.double() F64 ~126 GB — reserved / unreachable; never fabricated.

remaining_caveats: C3 attention reused at the mechanism level (tiny fixtures + MLA-0), not real-weight per-layer.
C1/C2 use a single deterministic probe input applied to every MoE layer's real weights (representative activation, not a real inter-layer trace) — consistent with the Qwen approach.
f32-vs-f64 DeepSeek block drift is looser than Qwen overall (documented ~1e-3 at full-forward), but per-expert C1 here is ~1.9e-6.
L1 here carries the C3-mechanism caveat; it is NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED, and NOT L2/L3/L4.

verification.reference: fixtures/moe/deepseek_v2lite_decomp_ref.safetensors (+ .json), float64 one expert at a time (~13.6 MB)
verification.expected: C1 global worst max_abs_diff ~1.9e-6 (< 0.5, all 1664 experts, layer 26 / expert 57); C2 top-6 set match on all 26 MoE layers; min routing margin 0.011981 (layer 23); 0 failures. Real run ~505 s.

generated_on: F64 reference: torch (bf16->float64) + NumPy float64, Windows 11. Atenia: CPU forward, RTX 4070 Laptop host, 32 GB RAM.
